title: crepis pannonica subsp. pannonica
intro: Crepis pannonica subsp. pannonica, also known as the Hungarian hawk's beard or Pannon hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Crepis pannonica subsp. pannonica include treating digestive issues, such as constipation and bloating, as well as alleviating symptoms of respiratory conditions like bronchitis and coughs. The plant's leaves and roots can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
